name: The Tyrant (M11S) - Official Adaptive Timeline
description: Standard mitigation-focused route for The Tyrant (M11S), curated from 10 aligned FF Logs clears plus guide cross-checking. Purely dodgeable mechanics have been stripped out so the timeline focuses on mitigation-relevant damage windows.

bossMetadata.name: The Tyrant (M11S)
bossMetadata.description: The ruthless overlord, third boss of AAC Heavyweight Tier (Savage).

guideSources.title: AAC Heavyweight M3 (Savage) Raid Guide for FFXIV
FFXIV Dawntrail AAC Heavyweight M3 (Savage) Guide

actions.name: Crown of Arcadia
actions.description: Opening raidwide. Use light party mitigation and healing before the first Trophy sequence begins.

actions.name: Impact
actions.description: Consistent physical party hit paired with the opening Raw Steel window. The aligned logs show this splash landing on the rest of the party often enough to keep it as a separate raidwide alongside the tank buster.

actions.name: Raw Steel
actions.description: Weapon-dependent physical tank damage check after the opener. Axe requires the tanks to share the buster while non-tanks spread; scythe cleaves both tanks plus a third player. Treat it as dual tank pressure.

actions.name: Trophy Weapons 1 Resolve 1
actions.description: First Trophy Weapons resolve after Assault Evolved. Clean pulls consistently show this as a full-party physical hit once the first weapon pattern lands.

actions.name: Trophy Weapons 1 Resolve 2
actions.description: Second Trophy Weapons resolve in the first weapon set. Treat this as a standard physical raidwide follow-up.

actions.name: Trophy Weapons 1 Resolve 3
actions.description: Third Trophy Weapons resolve in the opener sequence. This closes the first three-jump weapon set with another full-party physical hit.

actions.name: Comet / Crushing Comet
actions.description: Meteor resolve after the first Trophy Weapons set. Aligned logs show clean clears taking this as full-party magical damage, so keep party mitigation ready.

actions.name: Trophy Weapons 2 Resolve 1
actions.description: First Trophy Weapons resolve in the second weapon set. This is another full-party physical hit once the weapon pattern completes.

actions.name: Trophy Weapons 2 Resolve 2
actions.description: Second Trophy Weapons resolve in the second set. Use the same party mitigation plan as the earlier Trophy jumps.

actions.name: Trophy Weapons 2 Resolve 3
actions.description: Final Trophy Weapons resolve before the phase loops back into Comet and Crown of Arcadia.

actions.name: Comet / Crushing Comet
actions.description: Second Comet / Crushing Comet meteor resolve before the next Crown of Arcadia. Treat it as full-party magical damage.

actions.name: Crown of Arcadia
actions.description: Second full-party raidwide that bridges into Dance of Domination.

actions.name: Dance of Domination
actions.description: Major multi-hit physical raidwide sequence. The guide calls this seven total hits, with the final pulse hitting harder before the partner-stack follow-up.

actions.name: Eye of the Hurricane Resolve
actions.description: Follow-up full-party magical damage after Dance of Domination finishes. Heal and reset before the next Raw Steel window.

actions.name: Heavy Hitter
actions.description: Consistent physical party hit that lands with the second Raw Steel window. Cross-report evidence keeps this as a real raidwide on the rest of the party rather than sparse noise.

actions.name: Raw Steel
actions.description: Second Raw Steel tank check during the Dance of Domination loop. Both tanks are involved even though the exact pattern varies by weapon.

actions.name: Ultimate Trophy Weapons Resolve 1
actions.description: First Ultimate Trophy Weapons resolve. This six-jump sequence repeatedly lands full-party physical damage while the pattern rotates.

actions.name: Ultimate Trophy Weapons Resolve 2
actions.description: Second Ultimate Trophy Weapons resolve in the six-jump gauntlet.

actions.name: Ultimate Trophy Weapons Resolve 3
actions.description: Third Ultimate Trophy Weapons resolve in the gauntlet.

actions.name: Ultimate Trophy Weapons Resolve 4
actions.description: Fourth Ultimate Trophy Weapons resolve in the gauntlet.

actions.name: Ultimate Trophy Weapons Resolve 5
actions.description: Fifth Ultimate Trophy Weapons resolve in the gauntlet.

actions.name: Ultimate Trophy Weapons Resolve 6
actions.description: Final Ultimate Trophy Weapons resolve before Powerful Gust and the phase-end raidwide.

actions.name: One and Only
actions.description: Phase transition raidwide physical damage that ends the first phase and starts Meteorain.

actions.name: Great Wall of Fire
actions.description: Two-hit shared tank mechanic. The main tank takes the first hit in front and the off-tank fronts the second while the lingering line AoEs are dropped safely.

actions.name: Fearsome Fireball
actions.description: Repeated limited-party stack during Meteorain. A random non-prey player receives the stack, the front player takes extra damage, and the sequence repeats four times.

actions.name: Foregone Fatality
actions.description: Meteorain tank tether sequence. The guide describes these as magic tank busters with vulnerability that repeat three times.

actions.name: Flatliner
actions.description: Arena-split knockback that also deals physical damage to the full party. Mitigate it like a physical raidwide before the two-platform phase begins.

actions.name: Fire Breath
actions.description: Four closest players receive prey and are hit by line AoEs plus follow-up ground effects. Treat this as a four-player small-party damage event.

actions.name: Majestic Meteor 2
actions.description: Platform-split multi-hit stack marker, one on each platform. This is limited-party magic damage rather than a full raidwide and resolves over two grouped hits.

actions.name: Massive Meteor
actions.description: Grouped meteor barrage before the arena re-joins. Keep party mitigation up for the full four-hit physical sequence.

actions.name: Crown of Arcadia
actions.description: Raidwide at the start of the final phase loop.

actions.name: Great Wall of Fire
actions.description: Second two-hit shared tank mechanic in the final phase. Handle it the same way as the first Great Wall of Fire while keeping cooldowns for the enrage sequence.

actions.name: Crown of Arcadia
actions.description: Final-phase raidwide before Ecliptic Stampede resolves.

actions.name: Mammoth Meteor
actions.description: Opening Ecliptic Stampede proximity-style raid damage. Use party mitigation here before the targeted tower sequence begins.

actions.name: Majestic Meteor 2
actions.description: Second platform-split stack sequence after Ecliptic Stampede. Treat it as limited-party magic damage across both platforms.

actions.name: Crown of Arcadia
actions.description: Last full-party raidwide before Heartbreak Kick towers.

actions.name: Heartbreak Kick (Tower 1)
actions.description: First Heartbreak Kick tower. The central stack tower hits five times over the sequence, so plan sustained mitigation and healing.

actions.name: Heartbreak Kick (Tower 2)
actions.description: Second Heartbreak Kick tower. The tower gains one extra hit for a six-hit sequence.

actions.name: Heartbreak Kick (Tower 3)
actions.description: Final Heartbreak Kick tower at enrage. This last tower sequence expands to seven hits.

branches.label: Standard Route
branches.description: Curated mitigation-focused default route with dodgeable-only mechanics removed.

branches.label: Alternate Route A
branches.description: Minority branch detected in the first fight sample set. Metadata is preserved for future adaptive planners.

branches.label: Alternate Route B
branches.description: Early-diverging minority branch detected in the first fight sample set.

branches.label: Secondary Fight Route
branches.description: Primary branch detected in the second sampled fight index.

branches.label: Secondary Fight Alternate Route
branches.description: Minority route from the second sampled fight index.

analysisSources.title: FF Logs report API docs
analysisSources.notes: Existing curated timings remain aligned to the FF Logs clears used for the canonical adaptive route.

analysisSources.title: MitPlan Tyrant curation
analysisSources.notes: The existing mitigation-focused action library and branch structure are preserved; only planner-visible phase metadata is layered on top.

analysisSources.title: cactbot M11S timeline
analysisSources.notes: Shared milestone phase names are seeded from the cactbot section layout without regenerating the curated route or branch timings.
